note: Provisional VCG codes are AI-assigned for design purposes. CC assigns final database ids on apply. verse_assignments maps vc_id to provisional_code. Anchor verses marked with anchor_vc_id per VCG.

vcgs.label: Wrath as Covenantal Oath and Binding Consequence
vcgs.description: God's wrath as the settled inner disposition that grounds binding covenantal outcomes — oaths, exclusions, exile, and irrevocable verdicts. Wrath here has legal and covenantal weight: it grounds sworn oaths, bars from rest, and produces lasting territorial and relational consequences. The inner-being characteristic is wrath as settled moral verdict with covenantal bindingness.

vcgs.label: Wrath as Eschatological and Future Judgment
vcgs.description: Wrath understood as impending future divine force from which people flee, are rescued, or are delivered. The coming wrath that motivates repentance, from which Christ delivers, and which reaches its supreme expression in Revelation as cosmic final judgment. The inner-being characteristic is wrath as a future-facing judicial force creating urgency.

vcgs.label: Wrath as Active Judicial Force Against Ungodliness
vcgs.description: Wrath as an active, revealed, ongoing force operating against human sin — descending from heaven, accumulating against impenitence, produced by law's encounter with transgression, channeled through institutions, and falling on communities for covenant violation. Distinguished from VCG-02 by its present/historical operation rather than future-eschatological framing.

vcgs.label: Human Anger as Moral Response and Authority
vcgs.description: Anger in human persons — leaders, prophets, officials — as a morally grounded inner response to perceived disobedience, offense, or injustice. Anger as the inner state of moral authority when its order is violated. Includes the human anger that requires governance (Jam 1:19-20), Jesus's anger at hardness of heart, and the pattern of royal and prophetic anger.

vcgs.label: Wrath as Settled Abiding Condition
vcgs.description: Wrath understood as an ongoing, settled existential condition resting on those who refuse the Son — not a momentary reaction or future event but the permanent state of those who remain outside covenant obedience. Distinguished by its abiding, continuous quality contrasted with eternal life.

vcgs.label: Divine Burning Anger as Covenant Discipline
vcgs.description: God's burning inner heat directed against Israel's persistent covenant violation — the disciplinary fire that kindles at specific faithlessness and drives historical consequences: wilderness wandering, subjugation, and exile. Responsive to repentance and intercession. The inner-being characteristic is God's burning anger as a covenantal disciplinary heat.

vcgs.label: Human Anger Erupting into Outward Action
vcgs.description: Anger in human persons as an inner force erupting outward — driving speech, physical action, and punitive decisions. The transition from inner burning to outward act is immediate and direct. The inner-being characteristic is anger as an action-driving force: inner heat producing immediate consequential behaviour.

vcgs.label: Rage Filling and Possessing the Inner Person
vcgs.description: Anger as a force that floods and fills the inner person — welling to full capacity, taking possession, displacing other faculties temporarily, and visibly transforming the person. The inner-being characteristic is anger as a filling, possessing force that takes over until spent or satisfied.

vcgs.label: Righteous Human Anger Against Injustice and Folly
vcgs.description: Anger in human persons as a morally calibrated response to injustice, theological error, or moral failure — arising from conscience and covenantal concern, driving confrontation and intercession rather than impulsive action. The inner-being characteristic is anger as a conscience-grounded moral response.

vcgs.label: Restrained and Governed Anger
vcgs.description: Anger as an inner state present but requiring management and restraint — either by God whose compassion holds back wrath, or by wisdom commanding relinquishment. The inner-being characteristic is anger as a force subject to inner governance: present in the person but whose expression depends on will and wisdom.

vcgs.label: Divine Rage as Pouring and Spending
vcgs.description: God's burning inner rage through sustained imagery of pouring, spending, and releasing — a concentrated intensity that builds, is held back, and then is discharged completely. The inner-being characteristic is divine rage as a stored and discharged intensity: accumulating, releasing, exhausting itself through enacted judgment.

vcgs.label: Anger Experienced as Crushing Weight by the Recipient
vcgs.description: Anger understood from the perspective of the person bearing it — the inner experience of being under wrath: crushed, saturated, overwhelmed, and unable to stand. The inner-being characteristic is the experience of bearing anger as weight, saturation, and inner collapse.

vcgs.label: Divine Indignation as Sustained Moral Stance
vcgs.description: God's indignation as a persistent, principled inner moral posture — a standing disposition against wickedness, stored and purposefully timed, defining historical periods, and sometimes permanent toward specific offenders. The inner-being characteristic is indignation as God's standing moral verdict held as a determined, principled inner disposition.

vcgs.label: Human Indignation: Righteous Moral Anger Proving Inner Character
vcgs.description: Indignation in human persons as a morally revelatory inner response — the anger that proves genuine repentance (2Cor 7:11), and the dispositional anger-susceptibility that disqualifies from leadership (Tit 1:7). The inner-being characteristic is indignation as a moral inner indicator.

vcgs.label: Displeasure: Brooding and Situational Inner Discontent
vcgs.description: The softest anger register — sullen, brooding, situational displeasure that withdraws and shuts down rather than erupting. The inner-being characteristic is displeasure as a brooding closed-down inner state, the person in inner conflict with their circumstances.

vcgs.label: Idolatry Provoking Divine Anger
vcgs.description: The relational dynamic of human idolatry as an act that deliberately stirs God's anger — a covenant breach directed at his exclusive claim, provoking his inner anger through repeated, willful, and cumulative offense. The inner-being characteristic is idolatry as a directed relational provocation that arouses anger.

vcgs.label: Anger Aroused in Human Persons by Deliberate Provocation
vcgs.description: A human person's anger being deliberately or repeatedly stirred by another's targeted actions. The inner-being characteristic is anger aroused from the outside — a provoked inner state rather than self-generated.

vcgs.label: Spirit Provoked by Moral Encounter
vcgs.description: Anger arising spontaneously from within the inner person in response to what is encountered — not from deliberate targeting but from the conscience's encounter with idolatry, injustice, or wrong. The inner-being characteristic is spontaneous anger arising from moral perception.

vcgs.label: The Arrival of Anger: Inner Signal and Physical Expression
vcgs.description: The moment anger arrives in the inner person, evidenced through physical signals and behavioral collapse. The inner-being characteristic is anger's arrival as a perceptible inner event with somatic expression.

vcgs.label: Divine Jealousy as Constitutional Identity-Attribute
vcgs.description: God's jealousy as a defining constitutional attribute of his being — not a reaction to offense but a fundamental identity-level property: his very name is 'Jealous.' The inner-being characteristic is jealousy as God's exclusive-claim identity attribute from which all other expressions flow.

vcgs.label: Divine Protective Jealousy and Zeal for People and Name
vcgs.description: God's jealousy expressed as fierce protective passion for his people, city, land, and name — firing in their defense and motivating restorative action. The inner-being characteristic is jealousy as protective inner passion aroused by desecration of what is covenantally his.

vcgs.label: Human Zeal as Jealous Passion for God's Honor
vcgs.description: Human jealousy directed toward God — consuming zeal for his honor that identifies persons with his cause. Elijah, Phinehas, the psalmists consumed from within by zeal for God. The inner-being characteristic is human jealous passion directed upward: ardor for God's honor as personally at stake.

vcgs.label: Human Possessive Jealousy and Covetous Envy
vcgs.description: Jealousy in human persons directed at others — possessiveness, comparison-based resentment, covetous envy at another's advantage. Marital jealousy, sibling envy, inter-tribal rivalry, and the warning against envying sinners. The inner-being characteristic is possessive jealousy and covetous envy as a reactive comparison-based inner passion.

vcgs.label: Quarrelsome Disposition as Inner Character Flaw
vcgs.description: Anger as a settled inner disposition toward contention — a habitual orientation of the inner person toward dispute and quarreling. Strife as a work of the flesh, quarreling as an inner drive of the sinful nature, word-quarrels from inner craving for controversy. The inner-being characteristic is contentious anger as a dispositional feature of the person.

vcgs.label: Strife as Inner-Relational Contention
vcgs.description: Anger expressed as the relational atmosphere of inner contention — strife as the condition when pride and self-will go unchecked in relationship. Strife rooted in insolence, strife as an inner appetite aligned with sin, strife as corrupt inner motive underlying religious performance. The inner-being characteristic is anger as the relational output of inner pride and self-will.

vcgs.label: Aggregating VCG: Mixed-Register Terms Pending Researcher Disposition
vcgs.description: Aggregating placeholder VCG for 6 BOUNDARY terms designated at Phase 3 whose corpora were too mixed-register, thin, or edge-case for placement in characteristic sub-groups. Members: antilogia (mixed dispute registers), erethizō (constructive/damaging provocation, thin corpus), zestos (spiritual fervor not anger), ka.a.s (anger-register and grief-register split), riv (anger-driven contention and legal-procedural dispute split), tsur (no active relevant corpus — structural-only). Pending researcher disposition at Phase 12.
